                               T 
                            
                            
                               Y 
                            
                         
                      ,  
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               Y 
                               S 
                            
                         
                      ,  
                         
                            T 
                            
                               ( 
                               α 
                               ) 
                            
                         
                      , etc. In the more recent paper,  Ávila, Durán, and Moya (2013)  generalized these separations from topological spaces to knowledge structures, where the knowledge structure is called  
                         m 
                      -space. Due to space limitations, in this section, we take  
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               D 
                            
                         
                      -separation as a “separability” in knowledge structure to show that it appears in some well-studied knowledge structures. Recall that the dual of a knowledge structure  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                       is the knowledge structure  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            ¯ 
                         
                       containing all the complements of states in  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            ¯

                                  K 
                               
                               ¯

                               { 
                               K 
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  
                                     2 
                                  
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                               
                               
                               | 
                               
                               Q 
                               ∖ 
                               K 
                               ∈ 
                               K 
                               } 
                            
                         
                      .

To maintain consistency with naming,  
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               D 
                            
                         
                      -knowledge structures are called D-discriminative knowledge structures. It isknown that bi-discriminative  
                         ⟹ 
                       D-discriminative  
                         ⟹ 
                       discriminative (see  Ávila et al., 2013 ).

In their paper,  Aull and Thron (1962)  proved that a topological space  
                         X 
                       is a  
                         
                            
                               T 
                            
                            
                               D 
                            
                         
                      -space if and only if for every  
                         
                            x 
                            ∈ 
                            X 
                         
                       there exist an open set  
                         G 
                       and a closed set  
                         C 
                       such that  
                         
                            
                               { 
                               x 
                               } 
                            
                            = 
                            G 
                            ∩ 
                            C 
                         
                      .  Ávila et al. (2013)  pointed out that this property in knowledge structures is not true in general. But if the Maki’s condition is satisfied then the result is verified, where the Maki’s condition is  
                         ∪ 
                      -closed condition. More precisely, the following result was obtained from Theorem 11 of  Ávila et al. (2013) .

Lemma 6 
                         
                            Ávila et al., 2013 
                         
                         
                            A knowledge space  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is D-discriminative if and only if each singleton of  
                            
                               Q 
                             
                            is the intersection of an element of  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            with an element of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  ¯ 
                               
                            
                            .

A knowledge space  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is D-discriminative if and only if each singleton of  
                            
                               Q 
                             
                            is the intersection of an element of  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            with an element of  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  ¯ 
                               
                            
                            .

The concept of the learning space may be referred to  Falmagne and Doignon (2011) , where the axioms [L1] and [L2] were proposed by Falmagne to Cosyn and Uzun as offering a more compelling basis for learning space theory (see Page 39 of  Falmagne & Doignon, 2011 ).

Definition 18 
                         A knowledge structure  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is called a learning space if it satisfies the two following axioms. 
                         [L1] For any two states  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  L 
                               
                            , there exists a finite chain of states  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              0 
                                           
                                        
                                        ⊂ 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              1 
                                           
                                        
                                        ⊂ 
                                        ⋯ 
                                        ⊂ 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                        
                                        = 
                                        L 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            such that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        
                                           r 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                        
                                     
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                             for each  
                               
                                  i 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     1 
                                     , 
                                     2 
                                     , 
                                     … 
                                     , 
                                     p 
                                     } 
                                  
                               
                            , where each  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        r 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⁄ 
                                  ∈ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                        − 
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . 
                         [L2] If  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                               
                             are two states satisfying  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  L 
                               
                             and  
                               q 
                             is an item such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     q 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , then  
                               
                                  L 
                                  ∪ 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     q 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            .

A knowledge structure

                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             is called a learning space if it satisfies the two following axioms.

[L1] For any two states  
                               
                                  K 
                                  , 
                                  L 
                               
                             such that  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ⊂ 
                                  L

                                        K 
                                        =

                                              0 
                                           
                                        
                                        ⊂ 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              1 
                                           
                                        
                                        ⊂ 
                                        ⋯ 
                                        ⊂ 
                                        
                                           
                                              K 
                                           
                                           
                                              p 
                                           
                                        
                                        = 
                                        L 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            such that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                     
                                  
                                  =

It is known that every learning space is discriminative. However, a learning space need not be bi-discriminative (see  Example 1 ). Hence, the following result is provided.

                            -discriminative.

In their paper,  Cosyn and Uzun (2009)  proved that, for a knowledge structure  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                      , the axioms [L1] and [L2] were in fact equivalent to the hypothesis that  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a well-graded knowledge space. That is, the following result was obtained in  Cosyn and Uzun (2009) .

Lemma 7 
                         
                            Cosyn & Uzun, 2009 
                         
                         
                            A knowledge structure is a learning space if and only if it is a well-graded knowledge space.

A knowledge structure is a learning space if and only if it is a well-graded knowledge space.

By  Theorem 6  and  Lemma 7 , we have the following corollary.

Corollary 5 
                         
                            Every well-graded knowledge space is  
                            
                               D 
                            
                            -discriminative.

Every well-graded knowledge space is  
                            
                               D 
                            
                            -discriminative.

The following example shows that a well-graded knowledge structure need not be  
                         D 
                      -discriminative.

We do not know if there is an area of knowledge structure theory such that  
                         D 
                      -separability of items in the knowledge structure could be useful. Further, how to motivate and demonstrate usefulness of  
                         D 
                      -discriminative knowledge structures in knowledge structure theory? In addition, it is also interesting to characterize the  
                         D 
                      -separability of items in the delineated knowledge structures in terms of competencies of the skill multimap.

This paper has discussed the separability (resp. the bi-separability) of items in knowledge structures delineated by skill multimaps. We summarize the main results obtained in this paper and further communicate the relevance of the results and of the theoretical problem per se.

Based on these results, two interesting applications are obtained. First, it is investigated whether separability (resp. bi-separability) is preserved under particular constructions defined on skill multimaps. We show that both the prolongation and the submultimap of the skill multimap preserve the separability (resp. bi-separability) of items in the delineated knowledge structures. Second, around distributed skill functions and the meshing of the delineated knowledge structures, this paper discusses  Question 2  with respect to the separability and the bi-separability, where  Question 2  was posed by  Heller and Repitsch (2008) . More precisely,  Question 3  is discussed in this paper. As an answer to  Question 3 , under a given condition,  Theorem 5  has proved the equivalence between the separability (resp. bi-separability) of items in the parent structure and the separability (resp. bi-separability) of items in the substructures.

Furthermore, we raise some interesting questions.  Theorem 3  characterizes bi-separability of items in a knowledge structure which is delineated by a skill multimap. However, we do not know if there is a different characterization for skill functions. More precisely, we pose the following question: what is a necessary and sufficient condition weaker than  Theorem 3 (2), ensuring that knowledge structures delineated by skill functions are bi-discriminative? Next, under  Notation 1 , it is known that  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                       need not be a mesh of a meshable family  
                         
                            { 
                            
                               ( 
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               , 
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               ) 
                            
                            
                            | 
                            
                            i 
                            ∈ 
                            I 
                            } 
                         
                      . Accordingly, one can be interested in the following question: is there a necessary and sufficient condition such that the (consistent) family  
                         
                            { 
                            
                               ( 
                               
                                  
                                     Q 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               , 
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               , 
                               
                                  
                                     μ 
                                  
                                  
                                     i 
                                  
                               
                               ) 
                            
                            
                            | 
                            
                            i 
                            ∈ 
                            I 
                            } 
                         
                       is  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            S 
                            , 
                            μ 
                            ) 
                         
                      -consistent? Finally, we turn our attention to the following question, which is motivated by the proof of  Lemma 4 . The proof shows that “ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                               
                                  i 
                               
                            
                            
                            |

                       is pairwise disjoint” in  Lemma 4  can be relaxed to “ 
                         
                            {

                            } 
                         
                       is  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q

                            S 
                            , 
                            μ 
                            ) 
                         
                      -consistent”. As a reconsideration, we have two questions: (1) Can “ 
                         
                            { 
                            
                               
                                  S 
                               
                               
                                  i 
                               
                            
                            
                            | 
                            
                            i 
                            ∈ 
                            I 
                            } 
                         
                       is pairwise disjoint” in  Lemma 5  be weakened? (2) Can the assumption of  Theorem 5  be weakened?
